title: ESCALATE: QUOTA_API_KEY stored in plaintext in settings.conf

text: {"type":"escalate","finding":"QUOTA_API_KEY plaintext in settings.conf","severity":"Important","description":"The QUOTA_API_KEY setting in settings.conf stores the API key as plaintext. This is inconsistent with the server-side approach where --api-key-file is explicitly preferred. On the client side, the API key is readable by anyone who can read ~/.config/tmux-status/settings.conf.","options":[{"label":"Add QUOTA_API_KEY_FILE","solution":"Add a QUOTA_API_KEY_FILE setting that reads the key from a file, deprecate QUOTA_API_KEY","pros":"Consistent security model with server side","cons":"Adds config surface area, more complexity"},{"label":"Document chmod 600","solution":"Document that settings.conf should be chmod 600, add permissions check in renderer","pros":"Simple, pragmatic","cons":"Plaintext key still in config file"},{"label":"Accept as-is","solution":"Add a comment in settings.example.conf noting the trade-off","pros":"No code change, API key only protects local quota data","cons":"Inconsistent security posture"}],"recommendation":"Option 3 (Accept as-is). The API key protects local quota data, not upstream credentials. The session key (the actually sensitive credential) is already file-based with permission checking."}
